review_metadata.scope: Full governance and architecture audit — constitutional governance effectiveness, cross-repo enforcement, artifact contracts, scalability

findings.title: Compliance scanner checks file presence, not governance compliance
findings.description: The cross-repo compliance scanner (run-cross-repo-compliance.js) only verifies that required files and directories exist in downstream repos. It cannot detect contract version mismatches, schema incompatibility, governance section content violations, or architectural rule deviations. A downstream repo can pass all checks while implementing entirely incompatible contracts.
recommended_action: Extend the compliance scanner to validate contract version pins against contracts/standards-manifest.json.
Add checks for governance content in CLAUDE.md/CODEX.md (e.g., required sections, spectrum-systems references).
Validate that declared system_id matches ecosystem-registry.json entries.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Extend compliance scanner beyond file-presence to contract version validation

findings.title: Ecosystem registry missing 4 of 8 governed repositories
findings.description: ecosystem/ecosystem-registry.json lists only 4 repositories (spectrum-systems, system-factory, spectrum-pipeline-engine, spectrum-program-advisor). The four Layer 3 operational engines (comment-resolution-engine, working-paper-review-engine, meeting-minutes-engine, docx-comment-injection-engine) are absent. The registry cannot serve as an authoritative source of truth for the ecosystem.
recommended_action: Add all four missing operational engines to ecosystem-registry.json with accurate repo_type, status, and contracts fields.
Add a 'contracts' field to each registry entry listing consumed contract names for dependency tracking.
Add CI validation that cross-references registry entries against standards-manifest.json intended_consumers.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Complete ecosystem registry with all 8 governed repositories

findings.title: Production code boundary violation remains unresolved
findings.description: spectrum_systems/study_runner/ contains production pipeline code (pipeline.py, run_study.py, artifact_writer.py, load_config.py) in a repository whose CLAUDE.md states 'This repository should NOT contain production implementation code.' This was previously identified as RC-1 in the 2026-03-15 constitution audit. The artifact boundary CI check cannot detect Python source files — it only bans binary extensions.
recommended_action: Relocate spectrum_systems/study_runner/ to a dedicated engine repository.
Evaluate whether spectrum_systems/contracts/__init__.py should be reframed as a governance SDK or inlined in tests.
Remove run_study.py from repo root.
Extend check_artifact_boundary.py to detect production Python code in governance-only directories.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Remove production Python package from governance repo
findings.trigger: Previously identified as RC-1 — do not duplicate issue

findings.title: Dual schema authority creates confusion
findings.description: Schemas exist in both schemas/ (12 files, no manifest tracking, no test coverage) and contracts/schemas/ (16 files, manifest-tracked, validated by test_contracts.py). It is unclear which schemas are authoritative for downstream consumption. New contributors and AI agents may reference the wrong source.
recommended_action: Either consolidate all authoritative schemas into contracts/schemas/ with manifest tracking, or explicitly document schemas/ as internal/draft and add a README warning.
Add validation tests for schemas/ if those schemas are intended for downstream use.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Resolve dual schema authority between schemas/ and contracts/schemas/

findings.title: Contract consumer declarations are not verified
findings.description: contracts/standards-manifest.json declares intended_consumers for each contract, but no mechanism verifies that those consumers actually import, pin, or validate against the declared contract version. This is a governance declaration without verification.
recommended_action: As part of Phase 1 enforcement, require downstream repos to declare consumed contracts in a machine-readable manifest.
Cross-reference downstream manifests against standards-manifest.json intended_consumers to detect undeclared consumption or missing consumers.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Implement contract consumer verification against standards manifest

findings.title: Governance enforcement roadmap is entirely unstarted
findings.description: docs/governance-enforcement-roadmap.md describes a 4-phase enforcement model (declared identity → automated validation → CI conformance → ecosystem compatibility). All four phases are documented but zero are implemented. The gap between documented and enforced governance is the single largest structural risk.
recommended_action: Begin Phase 1 implementation: define the downstream contract pin format (e.g., governance.json or .spectrum-systems.json).
Extend the compliance scanner to validate contract pins against the standards manifest.
Set a target date for Phase 1 completion.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Implement Phase 1 of governance enforcement roadmap
findings.follow_up_trigger: When downstream contract pin format is defined and first repo declares pins

findings.title: No cross-repo CI enforcement exists
findings.description: All CI workflows run exclusively within spectrum-systems. No mechanism triggers governance validation when downstream repos change. Downstream repos can remove governance files, modify schemas, implement contracts incorrectly, or skip evaluation harnesses without any signal reaching the governance layer.
recommended_action: Add a scheduled CI workflow that runs the compliance scanner against all registered repos.
Publish compliance reports as CI artifacts with notification on drift.
Consider GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch or repository_dispatch for cross-repo triggers.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Add scheduled CI workflow for cross-repo compliance scanning
findings.follow_up_trigger: When compliance scanner is extended beyond file-presence (F-1)

findings.title: Artifact boundary check cannot detect content-based violations
findings.description: scripts/check_artifact_boundary.py bans binary file extensions and enforces file size limits but cannot detect Python source files or any content-based boundary violation. The check is structurally incapable of enforcing the 'no production code' rule.
recommended_action: Add a check for Python source files outside of tests/, scripts/, and spectrum_systems/contracts/ (if contracts/ is retained as a governance SDK).
Consider a simple allowlist of directories permitted to contain .py files.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Extend artifact boundary check to detect production Python code

findings.title: Review-to-issue pipeline lacks cross-repo deduplication
findings.description: ingest-claude-review.js can create issues in target repos via target_repo fields but has no deduplication logic. Running the ingest pipeline multiple times on the same actions file will create duplicate issues. No mechanism checks if an issue for a given finding ID already exists.
findings.recommended_action: Add deduplication by searching for existing issues with the finding ID before creation. Consider adding a 'created_issue_url' field to track which findings have been ingested.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Add deduplication logic to review-to-issue ingest pipeline

findings.title: Manual compliance scanning cannot scale beyond 5-7 repos
findings.description: The compliance scanner requires local checkouts of all repos, manual CLI invocation, and manual review of JSON output. At 15-30 repositories this process will be abandoned. The scanner must be automated with scheduled CI runs and notification on drift.
recommended_action: Automate the scanner in a scheduled CI workflow.
Publish compliance reports as GitHub Actions artifacts.
Send notifications (issue creation, PR comment, or external webhook) on non-compliance.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Automate cross-repo compliance scanning in CI

findings.title: Schema dual-track will create version confusion at scale
findings.description: With schemas/ and contracts/schemas/ coexisting without clear differentiation, new team members and AI agents will reference the wrong schema source. As the ecosystem grows, this ambiguity will compound into schema version mismatches across repos.
findings.recommended_action: Consolidate schema authority as described in F-4. This finding describes the scaling risk of the dual-track status quo.

findings.title: Review registry is a flat markdown table
findings.description: The review registry (docs/review-registry.md) tracks reviews in a markdown table. At 15-30 repos with quarterly reviews, this table will contain 60-120 rows within two years. Markdown tables do not support filtering, automated status updates, overdue item detection, or issue tracker integration.
recommended_action: Create a machine-readable review-registry.json alongside the markdown table.
Add CI validation that checks for overdue review actions by comparing due_date fields against the current date.
Use the JSON registry as the source of truth; generate the markdown table from it.
findings.suggested_issue_title: Create machine-readable review registry with CI-driven status checks

executive_summary: spectrum-systems has strong governance infrastructure within its own boundaries: schema-validated design reviews, 16 artifact contracts with test coverage, and CI-enforced artifact integrity.
Cross-repo governance is aspirational but not operational: the compliance scanner checks file presence only, the enforcement roadmap is unstarted, and the ecosystem registry is incomplete.
The repository violates its own boundary rule by containing production Python code — a credibility risk that must be resolved before mandating compliance from downstream repos.
Maturity assessment: 2.5 (Structured, approaching Governed). Six specific moves would advance the ecosystem to level 3.
strengths: Design review artifact system with paired markdown + JSON, schema validation, deterministic IDs, and review-to-issue pipeline.
16 artifact contracts with JSON schemas, example payloads, standards manifest, semantic versioning, and comprehensive pytest coverage.
Four CI workflows enforcing artifact boundaries, review artifact validation, review ingest, and project automation.
Clear layered architecture documentation with per-system design docs for all 9 systems.
Governance triage system with defined workstream buckets to prevent issue fragmentation.

gaps.statement: Cross-repo compliance scanning checks only file presence, not contract compliance or schema compatibility.
gaps.evidence: governance/compliance-scans/run-cross-repo-compliance.js checks requiredFiles and requiredDirectories arrays only.

gaps.statement: Ecosystem registry lists only 4 of 8 governed repositories — all four Layer 3 operational engines are missing.
gaps.evidence: ecosystem/ecosystem-registry.json contains spectrum-systems, system-factory, spectrum-pipeline-engine, spectrum-program-advisor only.

gaps.statement: Production code (spectrum_systems/study_runner/) violates the repository's own 'no production code' boundary rule.
gaps.evidence: CLAUDE.md states 'This repository should NOT contain production implementation code.' spectrum_systems/study_runner/ contains pipeline.py, run_study.py, artifact_writer.py, load_config.py.

gaps.statement: Dual schema locations (schemas/ and contracts/schemas/) create ambiguity about authoritative schema source.
gaps.evidence: schemas/ contains 12 files with no manifest tracking or test coverage; contracts/schemas/ contains 16 files tracked in standards-manifest.json with pytest validation.

gaps.statement: Contract consumer declarations in standards manifest are not verified against actual downstream usage.
gaps.evidence: contracts/standards-manifest.json declares intended_consumers but no tooling validates consumption.

gaps.statement: Artifact boundary check detects binary extensions only — cannot detect Python source code violations.
gaps.evidence: scripts/check_artifact_boundary.py BANNED_EXTS contains only binary/document extensions.

gaps.statement: Review registry is a flat markdown table that cannot support automated status tracking at scale.
gaps.evidence: docs/review-registry.md uses markdown table format with manual updates.

risks.statement: Downstream repos can silently deviate from governance rules without detection, causing contract incompatibility and schema drift across the ecosystem.
risks.rationale: No cross-repo enforcement exists. The compliance scanner only checks file presence. Contract consumer declarations are unverified.

risks.statement: Governance enforcement roadmap remains permanently aspirational while the ecosystem grows without enforcement.
risks.rationale: All four enforcement phases are documented but unstarted. Without a forcing function, enforcement will be deferred indefinitely.

risks.statement: Self-governance credibility erosion — downstream repos may resist compliance from a constitution that visibly violates its own rules.
risks.rationale: Production code in spectrum_systems/study_runner/ directly contradicts the stated boundary rule in CLAUDE.md.

risks.statement: Manual compliance scanning will be abandoned as the ecosystem scales beyond 7 repos, creating an enforcement vacuum.
risks.rationale: Scanner requires local checkouts and CLI invocation. No scheduled automation exists.

risks.statement: Schema version confusion across downstream repos as dual schema authority compounds at scale.
risks.rationale: Two schema directories with different governance levels create ambiguity about which source to use.

recommendations.statement: Close the self-governance gap: remove production code from the governance repo and extend boundary checks to detect Python source files.
recommendations.expected_outcome: spectrum-systems complies with its own boundary rule, establishing credibility for mandating compliance from downstream repos.

recommendations.statement: Complete the ecosystem registry with all 8 governed repositories and add contract consumption fields for dependency tracking.
recommendations.expected_outcome: The ecosystem registry serves as an authoritative source of truth for all governed entities and their contract dependencies.

recommendations.statement: Implement Phase 1 of the governance enforcement roadmap: define downstream contract pin format and validate against the standards manifest.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Downstream repos declare contract versions and those declarations are mechanically validated against the authoritative manifest.

recommendations.statement: Extend the compliance scanner from file-presence checks to contract version validation and governance content verification.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Compliance scanning detects contract drift, schema mismatches, and governance content deviations — not just missing files.

recommendations.statement: Consolidate schema authority by either merging schemas/ into contracts/schemas/ or explicitly documenting schemas/ as internal/draft with warnings.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Single clear source of truth for authoritative schemas; reduced confusion for downstream consumers and AI agents.

recommendations.statement: Evolve the review registry from markdown table to machine-readable JSON with CI-driven status and overdue checks.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Review tracking scales with ecosystem growth; overdue actions are automatically flagged.

recommendations.statement: Automate compliance scanning in a scheduled CI workflow with drift notification and report publication.
recommendations.expected_outcome: Compliance scanning runs automatically without manual invocation; drift is detected and reported without human initiation.

actions.title: Remove production code and extend boundary enforcement
actions.description: Relocate spectrum_systems/study_runner/ to a dedicated engine repo. Extend check_artifact_boundary.py to detect Python source files outside of allowed directories (tests/, scripts/). Remove run_study.py from repo root.
actions.expected_artifact: PR removing production code; updated check_artifact_boundary.py
acceptance_criteria: spectrum_systems/study_runner/ no longer exists in the repo
run_study.py no longer exists at repo root
check_artifact_boundary.py detects Python source files outside allowed directories
CI passes with the updated boundary check

actions.title: Complete ecosystem registry with all governed repos
actions.description: Add comment-resolution-engine, working-paper-review-engine, meeting-minutes-engine, and docx-comment-injection-engine to ecosystem/ecosystem-registry.json. Add a contracts field to each entry listing consumed contract names. Update the ecosystem-registry.schema.json if needed.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 governed repos appear in the registry
Each entry has repo_type, status, and contracts fields
test_registry_valid.py passes
Registry entries align with standards-manifest.json intended_consumers

actions.title: Implement Phase 1 governance enforcement
actions.description: Define a machine-readable downstream contract pin format (e.g., .spectrum-systems.json). Extend the compliance scanner to read and validate contract pins against contracts/standards-manifest.json. Document the format in docs/governance-enforcement-roadmap.md.
actions.expected_artifact: Contract pin format spec, extended compliance scanner, updated enforcement roadmap
acceptance_criteria: Contract pin format is defined and documented
Compliance scanner validates pins against standards manifest
At least one downstream repo has a valid contract pin file
Enforcement roadmap Phase 1 is marked as 'in progress' or 'complete'
actions.follow_up_trigger: When first downstream repo adopts contract pins

actions.title: Extend compliance scanner to validate governance content
actions.description: Enhance run-cross-repo-compliance.js to validate contract version pins, check CLAUDE.md/CODEX.md for required governance sections, and verify system_id against ecosystem-registry.json.
actions.expected_artifact: Enhanced compliance scanner with content validation
acceptance_criteria: Scanner validates contract pins (not just file presence)
Scanner checks for required CLAUDE.md/CODEX.md sections
Scanner cross-references system_id against ecosystem registry
Updated compliance-report.schema.json if report format changes

actions.title: Resolve dual schema authority
actions.description: Consolidate schemas/ and contracts/schemas/ by either moving all authoritative schemas into contracts/schemas/ with manifest tracking, or adding clear documentation and README warnings to schemas/ marking it as internal/draft.
actions.expected_artifact: Consolidated schema structure or clear documentation differentiating the two directories
acceptance_criteria: Each schema directory has a clear documented purpose
Authoritative downstream schemas are in one canonical location
schemas/README.md explicitly states whether schemas are for downstream consumption
If schemas are authoritative, they are tracked in standards-manifest.json and have test coverage

actions.title: Create machine-readable review registry
actions.description: Create docs/reviews/review-registry.json alongside the markdown table. Add CI validation that checks for overdue review actions by comparing due_date fields. Generate the markdown table from the JSON source.
acceptance_criteria: review-registry.json exists alongside the markdown table
CI validates JSON structure and flags overdue items
All existing registry entries are represented in JSON

actions.title: Automate cross-repo compliance scanning in CI
actions.description: Add a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow that runs the compliance scanner against all registered repos. Publish compliance reports as CI artifacts. Create issues or notifications on drift detection.
actions.expected_artifact: Scheduled CI workflow with compliance reporting and drift notification
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled workflow runs at least weekly
Compliance reports are published as CI artifacts
Non-compliance triggers notification (issue or alert)
Workflow handles repos that are unavailable gracefully

triggers: When A-1 (production code removal) is completed
Re-audit to assess maturity level advancement toward level 3 (Governed)
